subject: Biology
title: Leaves: Solar Panels of Nature
description: One-minute skill: Leaves: Solar Panels of Nature.

hook.text: A leaf is a solar panel engineered by evolution—flat, thin, and packed with chloroplasts.

buildup.text: The broad flat shape maximizes surface area for capturing light.

discovery.text: Stomata on the underside open and close to let CO₂ in and oxygen out while managing water loss.

twist.text: In autumn, trees stop making chlorophyll. Hidden pigments—yellow and orange—finally show through.

climax.text: Leaf shape varies wildly—needles in cold climates, broad blades in shady forests—each optimized.

punchline.text: Every leaf is a factory tuned by millions of years.

quiz.question: Why do leaves change color in autumn?
options: Chlorophyll breaks down, revealing hidden pigments
